mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment stays above 5.0 for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in retail sales and housing starts fail to generate positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of observed instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of elevated inflation triggers stagflation concerns that compress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of cases when industrial production falls while inflation exceeds 3.5 percent, or the interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0 percent imposes restrictive financing conditions that suppress corporate earnings growth, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns exceed zero in more than 65 percent of months when the funds rate remains above 4.0 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls strengthen household income expectations and support equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth over any 36-month rolling window, or the signal reverses direction during periods of rapid wage inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand relative to aggregate output, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDP-retail sales divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of observations, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production during a low interest rate environment signals economic weakness that prompts expectations of central bank stimulus, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain negative in more than 60 percent of instances when industrial production declines alongside sub-2 percent funding rates, or the regime condition fails to isolate the signal.
